features.title: Set up Playwright E2E testing
features.description: Install and configure Playwright for end-to-end browser automation testing. This is required before any feature work can be verified.
acceptanceCriteria: playwright.config.ts exists at repo root with baseURL set to http://localhost:3000
@playwright/test appears in package.json devDependencies
npx playwright install --with-deps chromium exits with code 0
A smoke test at e2e/smoke.spec.ts loads the app home page and asserts the page title is not empty
npx playwright test e2e/smoke.spec.ts --reporter=line exits with code 0

features.title: Update header text to 'Intentional Industries'
features.description: Locate the header component (likely in app/src/app/layout.tsx or a dedicated header component) and change the text content to 'Intentional Industries'. Ensure the text is visible and properly rendered.
acceptanceCriteria: Running `grep -r 'Intentional Industries' app/src/app/` finds the new header text in a layout or component file
The header text 'Intentional Industries' is present in the DOM when visiting http://localhost:3000 (can verify with curl or browser inspection)
No TypeScript compilation errors when running `cd app && npm run build`

features.title: Apply white text color to header
features.description: Add CSS styling to set the header text color to white (#ffffff or equivalent). This may involve updating app/src/app/globals.css, adding inline styles, or using Tailwind classes if the project uses Tailwind CSS.
acceptanceCriteria: The header element or its text wrapper has a CSS rule setting color to white (verify in app/src/app/globals.css or inline styles)
Visual inspection of the header shows white text (can be validated by checking computed styles in browser dev tools or screenshot comparison)
No CSS syntax errors when running `cd app && npm run build`

features.title: Apply horizontal gradient background (black to navy blue)
features.description: Add a CSS background gradient to the header element that transitions horizontally from black (#000000) on the left to navy blue (#000080 or similar professional navy shade like #001f3f) on the right. Use linear-gradient with appropriate color stops for a classy appearance.
acceptanceCriteria: The header CSS includes a background property with linear-gradient from black to navy blue (e.g., `background: linear-gradient(to right, #000000, #001f3f)`)
Running `grep -E 'linear-gradient.*black|#000.*navy|#001f3f' app/src/app/globals.css` or similar finds the gradient rule
The gradient direction is horizontal (left to right, specified as 'to right' or '90deg')
No visual artifacts or broken styling when viewing http://localhost:3000

features.title: Verify header styling in local development
features.description: Start the local development server using ./dev and visually confirm that the header displays 'Intentional Industries' in white text on a horizontal black-to-navy-blue gradient background. Ensure the styling looks professional and the gradient is smooth.
acceptanceCriteria: Running `./dev` starts the application without errors
Visiting http://localhost:3000 (or auto-incremented port) displays the header with white text reading 'Intentional Industries'
The header background shows a smooth horizontal gradient from black to navy blue
The header styling is consistent across different viewport widths (responsive design maintained)

features.title: Ensure production build compatibility
features.description: Run a production build of the Next.js application to ensure the header styling changes don't break the build process or cause runtime issues. Verify that the Docker image builds successfully with the new styling.
acceptanceCriteria: Running `cd app && npm run build` completes successfully with exit code 0
The production build includes the header styling changes (verify .next/static output contains the CSS)
Running `docker build -t test-build app/` completes without errors
The built Docker image, when run, displays the correct header styling at http://localhost:3000
